title: Lightly Fermented Portuguese Dough Flatbread
description: A traditional Portuguese dish featuring lightly fermented dough and gherkin relish, perfect for satisfying midnight cravings. This recipe is FODMAP friendly and made with simple ingredients.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine warm water, yeast, and sugar, and let it sit for 10 minutes until the yeast is activated.
Add flour, salt, and 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the bowl, and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it ferment in a cool place for 12 hours.
Preheat the oven to 400F and punch down the dough.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and brush with the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil.
Bake the flatbreads for 15 minutes until they are lightly browned.
Remove the flatbreads from the oven and top each one with gherkin relish.
Serve the flatbreads warm and enjoy.
